comment.body:
I wanted to quick chime in on Karen's reflection point and give the simple checklist I used to begin adding to short positions on Wednesday. 

1. Largest indicator to me was Friday, Mon, Tues volume on SPY, QQQ, IWM. All three had descending volume as price bounced extremely hard from last Friday's low. Alarming divergence. Then I saw the rangebound activity this Tues and Wed (which Alma mentioned to look for in her daily posts). 

2. SPY had rejected $695 multiple times before this week; Alma's weekly analysis for SPX matchd this level specficially the weekly upside pivot.

3. QQQ could hardly breach the 20 day VWMA - signaled to me sellers kept stepping in there. I believe NDX/IXIC/QQQ lead the way in selloffs with the current divergence so it was very key to pay attention to their price action.

4. Thursday's have been very steep reversal (or follow through) red days so I thought based on the above I thought it was a higher probability set up. 

5. This final one was key. Yesterday morning Alma had mentioned in the chat the butterly arbitrage and the negative smirk convexity. Whenever Alma says something premarket that is odd about the setup, I pay attention very keenly. And because this pointed to mispricing on the day, I added to short positions at open.

Hope that helps someone here. Happy Friday. Hope you all make some great gains today!
